messages.content: Helen Fisher 是谁
Helen Fisher 是一位美国人类学家、神经科学家和作家，以其对人类爱情和人类性行为的研究而闻名。她曾担任美国普林斯顿大学全球研究所人类学和人口研究项目的高级研究员。她是许多畅销书的作者，其中包括《为什么我们爱：神经科学揭示了我们的身体在恋爱中发生了什么》和《为什么我们欺骗：心理学、文化、神经科学揭示了人类欺骗本质》。她的研究和著作被广泛引用和讨论，并且在许多媒体上得到报道和评论。
Gerhard Schroder是谁

Gerhard Schröder 是德国政治家，曾担任德国总理。在他的政治生涯中，他也曾担任过德国联邦议院议员、国务部长和总理候选人。1998年，他领导社民党赢得德国联邦大选，并担任总理直到2005年。在他的领导下，德国实行了一系列经济和社会政策改革，包括减税和社会福利改革。他也是德国参与国际军事行动的支持者，包括参与科索沃战争和伊拉克战争。自离任总理后，他一直从事商业和咨询工作，并在国际上担任重要社交职务。
Jared Diamond是谁

Jared Diamond是一位美国科学家、作家和地理学家，出生于美国波士顿。他以其在人类学、生物学、生态学和地理学等领域的跨学科研究而著名。他的书籍《枪炮、病菌与钢铁》获得了普利策奖以及多个国际奖项，该书讨论了为什么有些社会和文明在历史上比其他文明更成功。他还撰写了许多关于生物多样性、环境保护和气候变化的著作，以及一些科学期刊的专业文章。他一直担任加州大学洛杉矶分校地理学教授，并曾被选入美国国家科学院、美国艺术与科学学院和美国哲学学会等多个著名学术组织。
转基因食品到底有害吗

转基因食品被广泛研究并发表了大量的论文，至今没有具有明确证据的的科学证明转基因食品对人类有害。然而，对于转基因食品的争议还是存在的，社会上对其的争议来源于对转基因食品的相关法规的成熟度以及对食品安全等问题的诸多疑虑。根据目前已知的科学数据，大多数转基因食品是安全的，但是在某些情况下，转基因技术也可能引起其他问题，如对环境的影响，可能破坏当地生态系统，或引入新的适应性优势等问题。因此，目前转基因技术的使用和实践需要进行更多的精细化规划以及反复的风险评估，用科学证据和实践效果来减少其存在的安全隐患。

翻译成中文:We tend to think of globalization as uniquely modern, a product of 20th century advances in transportation, technology, agriculture and communications. But widespread dispersal, from a few centers, of culture, language, political ideas and economic systems — even genetically modified 
foods — is actually quite an ancient phenomenon. 
2     The first wave of globalization began around 8500 BC, driven primarily by genetically modified foods created in the Mideast and China, and to a lesser extent Mexico, the Andes and Nigeria. As those foods spread to the rest of the world, so did the cultures that created them, a process that reshaped the ancient world in much the same way the U.S., 
Europe and Japan are reshaping today’s world.

我们通常认为全球化是现代独特的产物，是20世纪交通、技术、农业和通讯方面的进步的结果。然而，文化、语言、政治思想和经济体系的广泛传播，甚至包括转基因食品，实际上是一个相当古老的现象。第一次全球化浪潮始于公元前8500年左右，主要由中东和中国创造的转基因食品推动，较少程度的来自墨西哥、安第斯和尼日利亚。随着这些食物的传播到世界其他地方，创造它们的文化也随之传播，这个过程在很大程度上重塑了古代世界，就像今天的美国、欧洲和日本正在改变世界一样。

Our ancient ancestors’ method of genetically modifying food was of course much different from the way it is done today. When humans lived as hunters and gatherers, they had to make do with whatever wild plants and animals they found. It turned out, though, that some of the wild species upon which humans relied for food could be domesticated. Early farmers soon learned not only how to cultivate the resulting crops and raise livestock but also how to select the traits they valued, thereby genetically modifying foods. 
4     In choosing to sow seeds from wild plants with particularly desirable traits — often the result of mutations — early farmers changed genetically, albeit unconsciously, the foods 
they raised. Would-be farmers in some regions had a huge advantage. It turned out that only a few species of wild plants and animals could be domesticated, most of them native to the Mideast, China, Mexico, the Andes or Nigeria — precisely those places that became ancient centers of power. The crops and livestock of those five restricted homelands of 
agriculture still dominate our foods today.
6     Ancient people lucky enough to live in one of the few areas with wild plants that could be domesticated radically altered their societies. Hunters and gatherers traded their nomadic lifestyles for safer, more settled lives in villages 
near their gardens, orchards and pastures. Agricultural surpluses, like wheat and cheese, could be stored for winter
or used to feed inventors and bureaucrats. For the first time

我们远古祖先改良食物的方法当然与今天完全不同。当人类还是狩猎和采集时，他们必须利用找到的野生植物和动物。但事实证明，一些野生物种可以被驯化成为人类食物来源。早期农民不仅学会了如何种植这些植物和饲养家畜，还学会了如何选择他们所需要的特征，因此基因修改食物。选择从具有特定理想特征的野生植物中播种的种子 - 通常是基因突变的结果 - 早期农民基因上，虽然不自觉地改变了他们种植的食物。在一些地区想成为农民是有巨大优势的。事实证明，只有少数野生植物和动物物种能被驯化，其中大部分原产于中东、中国、墨西哥、安第斯或尼日利亚，恰恰是那些成为古代权力中心的地方。这五个农业限制家园的作物和家畜至今仍然主宰着我们的食物。有幸生活在少数几个能培育野生植物的地区的古代人彻底改变了他们的社会。狩猎采集者放弃了流浪的生活方式，搬到了靠近自己的菜园、果园和牧场的村庄里，过上了更加安全、更加稳定的生活。农产品的顺差如小麦和奶酪可以存储到冬天，或用来喂养发明家和官僚。这是人类历史上第一次

in history, societies could support individuals who weren’t directly involved in producing food and who therefore had time to govern or to figure out how to smelt iron and steel. As a result of all the extra food and stability, farming societies increased in population density a thousand fold over neighboring hunter-gatherers. Ultimately, ancient genetically modified foods conferred military and economic might on the societies that possessed them. It was easy for armies of 1,000 farmers, brandishing steel swords and led by a general, to kill or drive out small bands of nomads armed only with wooden spears. The result was globalization, as early farmers spread out from those first five homelands, carrying their genes, foods, technologies, cultures, scripts and languages around the world.

历史上，社会可以支持那些没有直接从事食物生产的人，他们因此有时间管理或研究如何冶炼铁和钢。由于所有额外的食物和稳定，农业社会的人口密度比邻近的狩猎采集者多了一千倍。最终，古代基因修改食品为拥有它们的社会带来了军事和经济的力量。拿着钢剑，由将军领导的一千名农民的军队很容易打败或赶走只有木制长矛的少数游牧民族。结果是全球化，早期农民从这五个家园扩散开来，带着他们的基因、食品、技术、文化、文字和语言走向全球。

It is because of this first wave of globalization that almost every literate person alive today uses one of only two writing systems: an alphabet derived from the first Mideastern alphabet or a character-based language that grew out of Chinese. This is also why more than 90% of people alive today speak languages belonging to just a half dozen languages of the five ancient homelands. The Indo-European family that includes English, for example, originated in the Mideast. But then as now, there was also a cost: Countless other ancient languages and cultures were eliminated as the early 
farmers and their languages spread.
8     The first wave of globalization moved faster along east-
west axes than along northsouth axes. The explanation is simple: Regions lying due east or west of one another share
相同的纬度，因此天气和季节变化相似，适合相同的植物和动物品种。在这些地区，转基因食品和其他技术的传播更加容易，也能够快速地发展和适应。但是，在南北方向上，人类栖息地的变化和气候的变化更为剧烈，导致相同的农业技术在不同的区域使用效果不一。因此，南北方向上的农业和经济发展一直落后于东西方向上的。

It is because of this first wave of globalization that almost every literate person alive today uses one of only two writing systems: an alphabet derived from the first Mideastern alphabet or a character-based language that grew out of Chinese. This is also why more than 90% of people alive today speak languages belonging to just a half dozen languages of the five ancient homelands. The Indo-European family that includes English, for example, originated in the Mideast. But then as now, there was also a cost: Countless other ancient languages and cultures were eliminated as the early 
farmers and their languages spread.
8     The first wave of globalization moved faster along east-
west axes than along northsouth axes. The explanation is simple: Regions lying due east or west of one another share

the same latitude, resulting in similar weather and seasonal changes, which make them suitable for the same plants and animal species. In these areas, the spread of genetically modified foods and other technologies is easier and can develop and adapt quickly. However, in the north-south direction, changes in human habitats and climate are more drastic, resulting in different effects of the same agricultural technologies in different regions. Therefore, agricultural and economic development in the north-south direction has always lagged behind that in the east-west direction.

As a result of this first wave of globalization, almost all literate people today use one of only two writing systems: an alphabet derived from the first Mideastern alphabet or a character-based language that originated in China. This is also why more than 90% of people today speak languages belonging to just a few language families originating from the five ancient homelands. For example, the Indo-European language family that includes English originated in the Mideast. But along with this benefit, there was also a cost: countless other ancient languages and cultures were eliminated as the early farmers and their languages spread.

翻译成中文: It is because of this first wave of globalization that almost every literate person alive today uses one of only two writing systems: an alphabet derived from the first Mideastern alphabet or a character-based language that grew out of Chinese. This is also why more than 90% of people alive today speak languages belonging to just a half dozen languages of the five ancient homelands. The Indo-European family that includes English, for example, originated in the Mideast. But then as now, there was also a cost: Countless other ancient languages and cultures were eliminated as the early 
farmers and their languages spread.
8     The first wave of globalization moved faster along east-
west axes than along northsouth axes. The explanation is simple: Regions lying due east or west of one another share

由于第一次全球化，今天几乎每个有文化的人使用的书写系统只有两种：一种是源自第一个中东字母表的字母表，另一种是源自汉字的基于字符的语言。这也是为什么超过90%的人今天说的语言属于五个古老家园的六个语言家族中的一种。例如，包括英语在内的印欧语系起源于中东。但是，像现在一样，这也是有代价的：随着早期农民和他们的语言的传播，无数其他古代语言和文化被消失殆尽。第一次全球化沿着东西方向比沿着南北方向移动得更快。原因很简单：东西方向上的地区共享相同的纬度，导致天气和季节变化相似，适合相同的植物和动物品种。在这些地区，转基因食品和其他技术的传播更加容易，也能够快速地发展和适应。但是，在南北方向上，人类栖息地的变化和气候的变化更为剧烈，导致相同的农业技术在不同的区域使用效果不一。因此，南北方向上的农业和经济发展一直落后于东西方向上的。
